columns.name: previous_day_admission_pediatric_covid_confirmed_0_4
columns.description: Enter the number of patients, by age group, who were admitted to an inpatient or ICU bed on the previous calendar day who had laboratory-confirmed COVID19 at the time of admission. The summary of age breakdowns should be identical to #18a. This includes patients ages 0-4, 5-11, and 12-17 years old admitted to any inpatient bed, regardless of whether the bed is designated as pediatric vs. adult. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. See Appendix D for the definition of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19.

columns.name: all_pediatric_inpatient_bed_occupied
columns.description: Total number of set-up and staffed inpatient pediatric beds that are occupied by a patient. Includes both PICU and med-surge beds (beds in which medical or surgical pediatric patients may be routinely placed). Include any occupied surge/hallway/overflow beds that are open for use. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #4a, and reflects occupancy levels for beds reported in #3c. This field is required as of 2/2/2022.

columns.name: all_pediatric_inpatient_bed_occupied_coverage
columns.description: Total number of set-up and staffed inpatient pediatric beds that are occupied by a patient (coverage). Includes both PICU and med-surge beds (beds in which medical or surgical pediatric patients may be routinely placed). Include any occupied surge/hallway/overflow beds that are open for use. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #4a, and reflects occupancy levels for beds reported in #3c. This field is required as of 2/2/2022.

columns.name: inpatient_beds_used_covid
columns.description: 15. Reported patients currently hospitalized in an inpatient bed who have suspected or confirmed COVID-19 in this state

columns.name: inpatient_beds
columns.description: 11. Reported total number of staffed inpatient beds including all overflow and surge/expansion beds used for inpatients (includes all ICU beds) in this state

columns.name: total_staffed_pediatric_icu_beds_coverage
columns.description: Total number of pediatric ICU beds in the facility that are currently set-up, staffed and are or could be used for a patient within the reporting period (coverage). This count includes occupied and unoccupied ICU beds, including any ICU beds that are, or could be, staffed and used for a pediatric patient. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #3c and #5a. Any beds counted in #5c should NOT be counted in #5b. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. Note: All pediatric ICU beds should be considered, regardless of the unit on which the bed is housed. This includes ICU beds located in non-ICU locations, such as mixed acuity units.

columns.name: staffed_pediatric_icu_bed_occupancy_coverage
columns.description: Total number of set-up and staffed pediatric ICU beds occupied by a patient (coverage). This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is subset of #4c and #6a. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. Note: All occupied pediatric ICU beds should be considered, regardless of the unit on which the bed is housed. This includes ICU beds located in non-ICU locations, such as mixed acuity units.

columns.name: previous_day_admission_pediatric_covid_confirmed_5_11_coverage
columns.description: Enter the number of patients, by age group, who were admitted to an inpatient or ICU bed on the previous calendar day who had laboratory-confirmed COVID19 at the time of admission (coverage). The summary of age breakdowns should be identical to #18a. This includes patients ages 0-4, 5-11, and 12-17 years old admitted to any inpatient bed, regardless of whether the bed is designated as pediatric vs. adult. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. See Appendix D for the definition of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19.

columns.name: all_pediatric_inpatient_beds
columns.description: Total number of pediatric beds in the facility that are currently set-up, staffed and able to be used for a patient within the reporting period. This count includes occupied and unoccupied inpatient pediatric beds including both PICU and med-surge beds (beds in which medical or surgical pediatric patients may be routinely placed). Include any surge/hallway/overflow beds that are open for use for a patient, regardless of whether they are occupied or available. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery beds, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #3a. This field is required as of 2/2/2022.

columns.name: staffed_icu_pediatric_patients_confirmed_covid
columns.description: Total number of pediatric ICU beds occupied by laboratory confirmed positive COVID-19 patients. This is a subset of #6c, occupied pediatric ICU beds. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. See Appendix D for the definition of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19.

columns.name: staffed_pediatric_icu_bed_occupancy
columns.description: Total number of set-up and staffed pediatric ICU beds occupied by a patient. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is subset of #4c and #6a. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. Note: All occupied pediatric ICU beds should be considered, regardless of the unit on which the bed is housed. This includes ICU beds located in non-ICU locations, such as mixed acuity units.

columns.name: total_staffed_pediatric_icu_beds
columns.description: Total number of pediatric ICU beds in the facility that are currently set-up, staffed and are or could be used for a patient within the reporting period. This count includes occupied and unoccupied ICU beds, including any ICU beds that are, or could be, staffed and used for a pediatric patient. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #3c and #5a. Any beds counted in #5c should NOT be counted in #5b. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. Note: All pediatric ICU beds should be considered, regardless of the unit on which the bed is housed. This includes ICU beds located in non-ICU locations, such as mixed acuity units.

description: After May 3, 2024, this dataset and webpage will no longer be updated because hospitals are no longer required to report data on COVID-19 hospital admissions, and hospital capacity and occupancy data, to HHS through CDC’s National Healthcare Safety Network. Data voluntarily reported to NHSN after May 1, 2024, will be available starting May 10, 2024, at COVID Data Tracker Hospitalizations. The following dataset provides state-aggregated data for hospital utilization in a timeseries format dating

name: COVID-19 Reported Patient Impact and Hospital Capacity by Facility
provider: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services

columns.name: hhs_ids
columns.description: As a part of an effort to improve the granularity of spatial data, unique identifiers (named “HHS IDs” in the datasets) have been assigned to each individual facility. These unique identifiers are provided so data users can reference each individual “brick and mortar” facility that is reporting data to HHS, even in cases when multiple facilities report under the same CMS Certification Number (CCN). Additional datasets and further details related to HHS IDs will be released at a later date.

Including those in observation beds. Including patients who have both laboratory-confirmed COVID-19 and laboratory confirmed influenza in this field.

columns.name: inpatient_beds_7_day_coverage
columns.description: [FAQ - 3. a)] Number of times in the 7-day period that the facility reported total number of staffed inpatient beds in your hospital including all overflow, observation, and active surge/expansion beds used for inpatients (including all ICU beds).

columns.name: total_staffed_pediatric_icu_beds_7_day_avg
columns.description: Average number of pediatric ICU beds in the facility that are currently set-up, staffed and are or could be used for a patient within the reporting period. This count includes occupied and unoccupied ICU beds, including any ICU beds that are, or could be, staffed and used for a pediatric patient. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #3c and #5a. Any beds counted in #5c should NOT be counted in #5b. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. Note: All pediatric ICU beds should be considered, regardless of the unit on which the bed is housed. This includes ICU beds located in non-ICU locations, such as mixed acuity units.

columns.name: staffed_icu_pediatric_patients_confirmed_covid_7_day_coverage
columns.description: Total number of pediatric ICU beds occupied by laboratory confirmed positive COVID-19 patients (coverage). This is a subset of #6c, occupied pediatric ICU beds. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. See Appendix D for the definition of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19.

columns.name: total_icu_beds_7_day_coverage
columns.description: [FAQ - 5. a)] Number of times in the 7-day period that the facility reported total number of staffed inpatient ICU beds.

columns.name: total_staffed_adult_icu_beds_7_day_coverage
columns.description: [FAQ - 5. b)] Number of times in the 7-day period that the facility reported total number of staffed inpatient adult ICU beds.

columns.name: staffed_icu_pediatric_patients_confirmed_covid_7_day_avg
columns.description: Average number of pediatric ICU beds occupied by laboratory confirmed positive COVID-19 patients. This is a subset of #6c, occupied pediatric ICU beds. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. See Appendix D for the definition of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19.

columns.name: previous_day_admission_pediatric_covid_confirmed_unknown_7_day_sum
columns.description: Sum of patients, by age group, who were admitted to an inpatient or ICU bed on the previous calendar day who had laboratory-confirmed COVID19 at the time of admission. The summary of age breakdowns should be identical to #18a. This includes patients ages 0-4, 5-11, and 12-17 years old admitted to any inpatient bed, regardless of whether the bed is designated as pediatric vs. adult. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. See Appendix D for the definition of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19.

columns.name: previous_day_admission_pediatric_covid_confirmed_5_11_7_day_sum

columns.name: all_pediatric_inpatient_beds_7_day_sum
columns.description: Sum of pediatric beds in the facility that are currently set-up, staffed and able to be used for a patient within the reporting period (coverage). This count includes occupied and unoccupied inpatient pediatric beds including both PICU and med-surge beds (beds in which medical or surgical pediatric patients may be routinely placed). Include any surge/hallway/overflow beds that are open for use for a patient, regardless of whether they are occupied or available. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery beds, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #3a. This field is required as of 2/2/2022.

columns.name: all_pediatric_inpatient_beds_7_day_coverage
columns.description: Total number of pediatric beds in the facility that are currently set-up, staffed and able to be used for a patient within the reporting period (coverage). Include any surge/hallway/overflow beds that are open for use for a patient, regardless of whether they are occupied or available. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery beds, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #3a. This field is required as of 2/2/2022.

columns.name: all_pediatric_inpatient_beds_7_day_avg

columns.name: total_personnel_covid_vaccinated_doses_all_7_day
columns.description: [FAQ - 44] The current number reported of healthcare personnel who have received a complete series of a COVID-19vaccination.Includes those who have received all doses in a multi-dose series as well as those who received a single-dose vaccine. (Collected on Wednesdays only)

columns.name: total_personnel_covid_vaccinated_doses_one_7_day
columns.description: [FAQ - 43] The current number reported of healthcare personnel who have received at least one dose of COVID-19 vaccination that is administered in a multi-dose series. This field is meant to represent those who have begun but not completed the vaccination process. Does not include those who received a single-dose vaccine in this field. (Collected on Wednesdays only)

columns.name: total_personnel_covid_vaccinated_doses_none_7_day
columns.description: [FAQ - 42] The number reported of healthcare personnel who have not yet received a single vaccine dose. (Collected on Wednesdays only)

columns.name: total_staffed_pediatric_icu_beds
columns.description: Total number of pediatric ICU beds in the facility that are currently set-up, staffed and are or could be used for a patient within the reporting period. This count includes occupied and unoccupied ICU beds, including any ICU beds that are, or could be, staffed and used for a pediatric patient. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #3c and #5a. Any beds counted in #5c should NOT be counted in #5b. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. Note: All pediatric ICU beds should be considered, regardless of the unit on which the bed is housed. This includes ICU beds located in non-ICU locations, such as mixed acuity units.

columns.name: all_pediatric_inpatient_bed_occupied
columns.description: Total number of set-up and staffed inpatient pediatric beds that are occupied by a patient. Includes both PICU and med-surge beds (beds in which medical or surgical pediatric patients may be routinely placed). Include any occupied surge/hallway/overflow beds that are open for use. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #4a, and reflects occupancy levels for beds reported in #3c. This field is required as of 2/2/2022.

columns.name: all_pediatric_inpatient_bed_occupied_coverage
columns.description: Total number of set-up and staffed inpatient pediatric beds that are occupied by a patient (coverage). Includes both PICU and med-surge beds (beds in which medical or surgical pediatric patients may be routinely placed). Include any occupied surge/hallway/overflow beds that are open for use. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #4a, and reflects occupancy levels for beds reported in #3c. This field is required as of 2/2/2022.

columns.name: all_pediatric_inpatient_beds
columns.description: Total number of pediatric beds in the facility that are currently set-up, staffed and able to be used for a patient within the reporting period. This count includes occupied and unoccupied inpatient pediatric beds including both PICU and med-surge beds (beds in which medical or surgical pediatric patients may be routinely placed). Include any surge/hallway/overflow beds that are open for use for a patient, regardless of whether they are occupied or available. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery beds, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #3a. This field is required as of 2/2/2022.

columns.name: state_fips
columns.description: 3. state_fips (string) - Numerical identifier of state associated with the test. Typically patient's state of residence, but provider or lab state used when patient is unavailable.

columns.name: fema_region
columns.description: 4. fema_region (string) - Region associated with the test. Typically that of patient's state of residence, but provider or lab state used when patient is unavailable.

columns.name: overall_outcome
columns.description: 5. overall_outcome (string) - Outcome of test -- Positive, Negative or Inconclusive.

columns.name: state_name
columns.description: 2. state_name (string) - Name of state associated with the test. Typically patient's state of residence, but provider or lab state used when patient is unavailable.

columns.name: new_results_reported
columns.description: 7. new_results_reported (long) - The number of tests completed with the specified outcome in the specified state on the listed date. (Large spikes may result from states submitting tests for several proceeding days at once with a single date).

columns.name: total_results_reported
columns.description: 8. total_results_reported (long) - The cumulative number of tests completed with the specified outcome in the specified state up through the listed date.

columns.name: state
columns.description: 1. state (string) - Abbreviation of state associated with the test. Typically patient's state of residence, but provider or lab state used when patient is unavailable.

columns.name: date
columns.description: 6. date (date) - Typically the date the test completed or the date that the result was reported back to the patient. If neither are available, it can be the date the specimen was collected, arrived at the testing facility, or the date the test was ordered.

description: This data represents unaccompanied alien children who are taken into custody by Customs and Border Protection brought to a facility and processed for transfer to the Department of Health and Human Services (HHS) as required by law. HHS holds the child for testing and quarantine, and shelters the child until the child is placed with a sponsor here in the United States.

columns.name: all_adult_hospital_inpatient_beds_7_day_coverage
columns.description: [FAQ - 3. b)] Number of times in the 7-day period that the facility reported total number of staffed inpatient adult beds in your hospital including all overflow and active surge/expansion beds used for inpatients (including all designated ICU beds).

columns.name: all_adult_hospital_beds_7_day_coverage
columns.description: [FAQ - 2. b)] Number of times in the 7-day period that the facility reported total number of all staffed inpatient and outpatient adult beds in your hospital, including all overflow and active surge/expansion beds for inpatients and for outpatients (including all ICU, ED, and observation).

columns.name: total_beds_7_day_coverage
columns.description: [FAQ - 2. a)] Number of times in the 7 day period that the facility reported total number of all staffed inpatient and outpatient beds in your hospital, including all overflow, observation, and active surge/expansion beds used for inpatients and for outpatients (including all ICU, ED, and observation).

columns.name: inpatient_beds_used_covid_7_day_sum
columns.description: Sum of reported patients currently hospitalized in an inpatient bed who have suspected or confirmed COVID-19 reported during the 7-day period.

columns.name: total_adult_patients_hospitalized_confirmed_and_suspected_covid_7_day_sum
columns.description: [FAQ - 9. a)] Sum of reports of patients currently hospitalized in an adult inpatient bed who have laboratory-confirmed or suspected COVID19. Including those in observation beds reported during the 7-day period.

columns.name: total_patients_hospitalized_confirmed_influenza_and_covid_7_day_sum
columns.description: [FAQ - 36] Sum of reports of patients (all ages) currently hospitalized in an inpatient bed who have laboratory-confirmed COVID-19 and laboratory-confirmed influenza reported in the 7-day period.

columns.name: icu_patients_confirmed_influenza_7_day_sum
columns.description: [FAQ - 35] Sum of reports of patients (all ages) currently hospitalized in a designated ICU bed with laboratory-confirmed influenza reported in the 7-day period.

columns.name: total_patients_hospitalized_confirmed_influenza_7_day_sum
columns.description: [FAQ - 33] Sum of reports of patients (all ages) currently hospitalized in an inpatient bed who have laboratory-confirmed influenza. Including those in observation beds reported in the 7-day period.

columns.name: previous_day_admission_pediatric_covid_confirmed_12_17_7_day_sum
columns.description: Sum of patients, by age group, who were admitted to an inpatient or ICU bed on the previous calendar day who had laboratory-confirmed COVID19 at the time of admission. The summary of age breakdowns should be identical to #18a. This includes patients ages 0-4, 5-11, and 12-17 years old admitted to any inpatient bed, regardless of whether the bed is designated as pediatric vs. adult. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. See Appendix D for the definition of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19.

columns.name: staffed_pediatric_icu_bed_occupancy_7_day_coverage
columns.description: Total number of set-up and staffed pediatric ICU beds occupied by a patient (coverage). This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is subset of #4c and #6a. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. Note: All occupied pediatric ICU beds should be considered, regardless of the unit on which the bed is housed. This includes ICU beds located in non-ICU locations, such as mixed acuity units.

columns.name: previous_day_admission_pediatric_covid_confirmed_5_11_7_day_sum
columns.description: Sum of patients, by age group, who were admitted to an inpatient or ICU bed on the previous calendar day who had laboratory-confirmed COVID19 at the time of admission. The summary of age breakdowns should be identical to #18a. This includes patients ages 0-4, 5-11, and 12-17 years old admitted to any inpatient bed, regardless of whether the bed is designated as pediatric vs. adult. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. See Appendix D for the definition of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19.

columns.name: staffed_pediatric_icu_bed_occupancy_7_day_avg
columns.description: Average of set-up and staffed pediatric ICU beds occupied by a patient. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is subset of #4c and #6a. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. Note: All occupied pediatric ICU beds should be considered, regardless of the unit on which the bed is housed. This includes ICU beds located in non-ICU locations, such as mixed acuity units.

columns.name: staffed_icu_pediatric_patients_confirmed_covid_7_day_coverage
columns.description: Total number of pediatric ICU beds occupied by laboratory confirmed positive COVID-19 patients (coverage). This is a subset of #6c, occupied pediatric ICU beds. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. See Appendix D for the definition of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19.

columns.name: all_pediatric_inpatient_beds_7_day_sum
columns.description: Sum of pediatric beds in the facility that are currently set-up, staffed and able to be used for a patient within the reporting period (coverage). This count includes occupied and unoccupied inpatient pediatric beds including both PICU and med-surge beds (beds in which medical or surgical pediatric patients may be routinely placed). Include any surge/hallway/overflow beds that are open for use for a patient, regardless of whether they are occupied or available. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery beds, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #3a. This field is required as of 2/2/2022.

columns.name: previous_day_admission_pediatric_covid_confirmed_0_4_7_day_sum
columns.description: Sum of patients, by age group, who were admitted to an inpatient or ICU bed on the previous calendar day who had laboratory-confirmed COVID19 at the time of admission. The summary of age breakdowns should be identical to #18a. This includes patients ages 0-4, 5-11, and 12-17 years old admitted to any inpatient bed, regardless of whether the bed is designated as pediatric vs. adult. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. See Appendix D for the definition of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19.

columns.name: all_pediatric_inpatient_beds_7_day_coverage
columns.description: Total number of pediatric beds in the facility that are currently set-up, staffed and able to be used for a patient within the reporting period (coverage). This count includes occupied and unoccupied inpatient pediatric beds including both PICU and med-surge beds (beds in which medical or surgical pediatric patients may be routinely placed). Include any surge/hallway/overflow beds that are open for use for a patient, regardless of whether they are occupied or available. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery beds, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #3a. This field is required as of 2/2/2022.

columns.name: all_pediatric_inpatient_bed_occupied_7_day_coverage
columns.description: Total number of set-up and staffed inpatient pediatric beds that are occupied by a patient (coverage). Includes both PICU and med-surge beds (beds in which medical or surgical pediatric patients may be routinely placed). Include any occupied surge/hallway/overflow beds that are open for use. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #4a, and reflects occupancy levels for beds reported in #3c. This field is required as of 2/2/2022.

columns.name: hospital_pk
columns.description: This unique key for the given hospital that will match the ccn column if it exists, otherwise, it is a derived unique key.

columns.name: total_staffed_pediatric_icu_beds_7_day_avg
columns.description: Average number of pediatric ICU beds in the facility that are currently set-up, staffed and are or could be used for a patient within the reporting period. This count includes occupied and unoccupied ICU beds, including any ICU beds that are, or could be, staffed and used for a pediatric patient. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #3c and #5a. Any beds counted in #5c should NOT be counted in #5b. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. Note: All pediatric ICU beds should be considered, regardless of the unit on which the bed is housed. This includes ICU beds located in non-ICU locations, such as mixed acuity units.

columns.name: collection_week
columns.description: This date indicates the start of the period of reporting (the starting Friday).

columns.name: state
columns.description: [FAQ - 1. d)] The two digit state/territory code for the hospital.

columns.name: ccn
columns.description: [FAQ - 1. b)] CMS Certification Number (CCN) of the given facility

columns.name: hospital_name
columns.description: [FAQ - 1. a)] The name of the facility reporting.

This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #4a, and reflects occupancy levels for beds reported in #3c. This field is required as of 2/2/2022.

Note: All pediatric ICU beds should be considered, regardless of the unit on which the bed is housed. This includes ICU beds located in non-ICU locations, such as mixed acuity units.

columns.description: Sum of pediatric ICU beds in the facility that are currently set-up, staffed and are or could be used for a patient within the reporting period. This count includes occupied and unoccupied ICU beds, including any ICU beds that are, or could be, staffed and used for a pediatric patient. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #3c and #5a. Any beds counted in #5c should NOT be counted in #5b. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. Note: All pediatric ICU beds should be considered, regardless of the unit on which the bed is housed. This includes ICU beds located in non-ICU locations, such as mixed acuity units.

columns.description: Average number of pediatric ICU beds occupied by laboratory confirmed positive COVID-19 patients. This is a subset of #6c, occupied pediatric ICU beds. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. See Appendix D for the definition of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19.

columns.name: previous_week_patients_covid_vaccinated_doses_all_7_day
columns.description: [FAQ - 47] The number of patients who received the final dose in a COVID-19 vaccination series. (This field is only reported on Wednesdays of a given week)

columns.name: previous_week_patients_covid_vaccinated_doses_one_7_day
columns.description: [FAQ - 46] The number of patients who received the first dose of a COVID-19 vaccine that is administered in a multi-dose series. (This field is only reported on Wednesdays of a given week)

columns.name: total_personnel_covid_vaccinated_doses_all_7_day
columns.description: [FAQ - 44] The current number of healthcare personnel who have received a complete series of a COVID-19vaccination.Includes those who have received all doses in a multi-dose series as well as those who received a single-dose vaccine. (This field is only reported on Wednesdays of a given week)

columns.name: total_personnel_covid_vaccinated_doses_one_7_day
columns.description: [FAQ - 43] The current maximum number of healthcare personnel who have received at least one dose of COVID-19 vaccination that is administered in a multi-dose series. This field is meant to represent those who have begun but not completed the vaccination process. Does not include those who received a single-dose vaccine in this field. (This field is only reported on Wednesdays of a given week)

columns.name: total_personnel_covid_vaccinated_doses_none_7_day
columns.description: [FAQ - 42] The number reported of healthcare personnel who have not yet received a single vaccine dose. (This field is only reported on Wednesdays of a given week)

columns.name: previous_week_personnel_covid_vaccinated_doses_administered_7_day
columns.description: [FAQ - 41] The number reported of COVID-19 vaccination doses administered in the previous week regardless of whether it is a first or second dose in a series. (This field is only reported on Wednesdays of a given week)

columns.name: previous_day_admission_pediatric_covid_suspected_7_day_coverage

columns.name: staffed_pediatric_icu_bed_occupancy_7_day_sum
columns.description: Sum of set-up and staffed pediatric ICU beds occupied by a patient. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is subset of #4c and #6a. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. Note: All occupied pediatric ICU beds should be considered, regardless of the unit on which the bed is housed. This includes ICU beds located in non-ICU locations, such as mixed acuity units.

columns.name: previous_day_admission_adult_covid_suspected_7_day_coverage
columns.description: [FAQ - 17b] Number of times in the 7 day period that the facility reported the number of patients admitted to adult inpatient bed on previous calendar day with suspected COVID-19

columns.name: previous_day_admission_pediatric_covid_confirmed_7_day_coverage

columns.name: hhs_ids
columns.description: As a part of an effort to improve the granularity of spatial data, unique identifiers (named “HHS IDs” in the datasets) have been assigned to each individual facility. These unique identifiers are provided so data users can reference each individual “brick and mortar” facility that is reporting data to HHS, even in cases when multiple facilities report under the same CMS Certification Number (CCN). Additional datasets and further details related to HHS IDs will be released at a later date.

columns.name: geocoded_hospital_address

name: COVID-19 State and County Policy Orders
provider: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services

columns.name: fips_code
columns.description: 3. fips_code (string): A list of fips codes where the policy change is occurring. This is the authoritative column for where a county policy change occurs.

columns.name: total_phases
columns.description: 10. total_phases (integer): As some states shifted to reopening policies, this column captures the number of phases of reopening that were expected.

columns.name: state_id
columns.description: 1. state_id (string): The two digit state code for the policy change

columns.name: source
columns.description: 9. source (string): Where this policy comes from. Either BU COVID-19 Policy Database, Wikidata, manual (no source), or sip_submission_form (with source website URL).

columns.name: comments
columns.description: 8. comments (string): Details about the policies. In some cases, simply a link to a website with more information.

columns.name: start_stop
columns.description: 7. start_stop (string): Either “start” or “stop” Indicating whether this entry is for the beginning or end of a policy, respectively.

columns.name: date
columns.description: 5. date (date, formatted YYYY-MM-dd): The date of the policy. Please note that there is also a start and stop column, to indicate whether this date is for the beginning or end of the policy.

columns.name: policy_type
columns.description: 6. policy_type (string): The “type” of policy this in. Generically fitted in to many categories including Gym policies, Food and Drink policies, Stay at Home policies, and more.

columns.name: county
columns.description: 2. county (optional string): The county name where the policy change was implemented. Optional due to counties occasionally implementing their own policies.

columns.name: policy_level
columns.description: 4. policy_level (string): Whether the policy is a state of county-level policy. Either “state” or “county.”

(2020). "COVID-19 US state policy database." wikidata - Stay At Home Policies Queried from WikiData Manual curation by a dedicated group of Virtual Student Federal Service Interns - Summer 2020: T Adler, J Bastian, L Beckett

provider: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

columns.name: ZIP Code
columns.description: School district zip code (NCES 2020-21)

columns.description: School district city (NCES 2020-21)

columns.name: Learning Modality
columns.description: The learning modality of a given school district which includes in-person, remote, or hybrid learning. In-Person: All schools within the district offer face-to-face instruction 5 days per week to all students at all available grade levels. Remote: Schools within the district do not offer face-to-face instruction; all learning is conducted online/remotely to all students at all available grade levels. Hybrid: Schools within the district offer a combination of in-person and remote learning; face-to-face instruction is offered less than 5 days per week, or only to a subset of students.

columns.name: Student Count
columns.description: Number of students enrolled in each district (NCES 2020-21)

columns.description: School district name (NCES 2020-21)

columns.name: Operational Schools
columns.description: Number of schools in each district (NCES 2020-21)

description: The 2021-2022 School Learning Modalities dataset provides weekly estimates of school learning modality (including in-person, remote, or hybrid learning) for U.S. K-12 public and independent charter school districts for the 2021-2022 school year and the Fall 2022 semester, from August 2021 – December 2022. name: COVID-19 20220110 FAQ Template v10

columns.name: critical_staffing_shortage_anticipated_within_week
columns.description: Enter Y if you anticipate a critical staffing shortage within a week. Enter N if you do not anticipate a staffing shortage within a week. If you do not report this value, the default is N. If you have a shortage, report Y until the shortage is resolved. Each facility should identify staffing shortages based on their facility needs and internal policies for staffing ratios. The use of temporary staff does not count as a staffing shortage if staffing ratios are met according to the facility’s needs and internal policies for staffing ratios.

columns.name: previous_week_patients_covid_vaccinated_doses_one
columns.description: For the previous week, enter the number of patients and other non-healthcare personnel, defined as any person who is not a healthcare worker at your facility, who received the first dose in a multi-series of COVID-19 vaccination doses administered in your facility. The vaccination can occur for an inpatient, outpatient, or community clinic visitor if the doses were allocated to and administered by the facility. This field is meant to represent those who have begun but not completed the vaccination process. Do not include those who have had the second or final dose of a multi-dose vaccination series or those who have had a single-dose vaccination. For the first week of reporting, include all doses given up to that date.

columns.name: total_personnel_covid_vaccinated_doses_none
columns.description: Enter the number of healthcare personnel serving your facility, who have not yet received any kind of vaccine dose (first in a multi-series, single-dose vaccine, etc.). This field is meant to represent personnel who have not had a single vaccine dose yet regardless of where the vaccine is administered. (your facility or elsewhere).

columns.name: total_personnel
columns.description: Enter the current total number of healthcare personnel serving your facility. Healthcare personnel include all paid and unpaid persons serving in your healthcare setting who have the potential for direct or indirect exposure to patients or infectious materials.

columns.name: previous_week_Therapeutic_I_courses_used

columns.description: Enter the number of patients, by age group, who were admitted to an inpatient or ICU bed on the previous calendar day who had laboratory-confirmed COVID19 at the time of admission. The summary of age breakdowns should be identical to #18a. This includes patients ages 0-4, 5-11, and 12-17 years old admitted to any inpatient bed, regardless of whether the bed is designated as pediatric vs. adult. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. See Appendix D for the definition of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19.

columns.name: staffed_icu_pediatric_patients_confirmed_covid
columns.description: Total number of pediatric ICU beds occupied by laboratory confirmed positive COVID-19 patients. This is a subset of #6c, occupied pediatric ICU beds. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. See Appendix D for the definition of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19.

columns.name: total_personnel_covid_vaccinated_doses_all
columns.description: Enter the current total number of healthcare personnel serving your facility, who are fully vaccinated, defined as having received a completed series of a COVID-19 vaccination or a single-dose vaccination. This field is meant to represent those who have completed the COVID-19 vaccination process, regardless of where it was administered (your facility or elsewhere) and regardless of how many doses are needed to complete the vaccination series.

columns.name: previous_day_admission_pediatric_covid_confirmed_unknown

The summary of age breakdowns should be identical to #18a. This includes patients ages 0-4, 5-11, and 12-17 years old admitted to any inpatient bed, regardless of whether the bed is designated as pediatric vs. adult. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. See Appendix D for the definition of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19.

columns.name: previous_week_personnel_covid_vaccinated_doses_administered
columns.description: For the previous week, enter the number of COVID19 vaccination doses administered to any healthcare personnel in the previous week by your facility. Enter the count of all doses administered (first in a multi-series vaccine, second or final in a multi-series vaccine, or single-dose vaccine). For the first week of reporting, include all dose given up to that date.

See Appendix D for the definition of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19.

columns.name: all_pediatric_inpatient_bed_occupied
columns.description: Total number of set-up and staffed inpatient pediatric beds that are occupied by a patient. Includes both PICU and med-surge beds (beds in which medical or surgical pediatric patients may be routinely placed). Include any occupied surge/hallway/overflow beds that are open for use. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #4a, and reflects occupancy levels for beds reported in #3c. This field is required as of 2/2/2022.

columns.name: on_hand_supply_of_eye_protection_in_days
columns.description: Provide calculated range of days of supply in stock for each PPE category. For supply categories that may have varying quantities or days on hand, report the days on hand for the item that has the lowest stock on hand. • 0 days • 1-3 days • 4-6 days • 7-14 days • 15-30 days • >30 days Calculations may be provided by your hospital’s ERP system or by utilizing the CDC’s PPE burn rate calculator assumptions.

columns.name: total_staffed_pediatric_icu_beds
columns.description: Total number of pediatric ICU beds in the facility that are currently set-up, staffed and are or could be used for a patient within the reporting period. This count includes occupied and unoccupied ICU beds, including any ICU beds that are, or could be, staffed and used for a pediatric patient. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #3c and #5a. Any beds counted in #5c should NOT be counted in #5b. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. Note: All pediatric ICU beds should be considered, regardless of the unit on which the bed is housed. This includes ICU beds located in non-ICU locations, such as mixed acuity units.

description: Template for requested daily data reports on testing, capacity and utilization, and patient flows to facilitate the public health response to the 2019 Novel Coronavirus (COVID-19).

name: Perpetrators by Relationship to Their Victims
provider: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services / ACF

columns.name: Friend and Neighbor
columns.description: The number of perpetrators (unique count) with a relationship to their victim(s) of friend and neighbor, which is defined as a nonrelative acquainted with the child, the parent, or caregiver.  For more information and definitions of relationships, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Child Daycare Provider
columns.description: The number of perpetrators (unique count) with a relationship to their victim(s) of child daycare provider, which is defined as a person with a temporary caregiver responsibility, but who is not related to the child, such as a daycare center staff member, family provider, or babysitter. For more information and definitions of relationships, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Other
columns.description: The number of perpetrators (unique count) with a relationship to their victim(s) of “other,” which is used when the state coding for this field is not one of the codes in the NCANDS record layout. For more information and definitions of relationships, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: State
columns.description: The primary unit from which child maltreatment data are collected. This includes all 50 states, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and the District of Columbia. The state column also includes a national row which displays the total sum, percentage, or rate for only those states included in the analysis.

columns.name: Foster Parent
columns.description: The number of perpetrators (unique count) with a relationship to their victim(s) of foster parent, which is defined as an individual who provides a home for orphaned, abused, neglected, delinquent, or disabled children under the placement, care, or supervision of the state. For more information and definitions of relationships, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Group Home and Residential Facility Staff
columns.description: The number of perpetrators (unique count) with a relationship to their victim(s) of group home and residential facility staff, which is defined as an employee of a nonfamilial 24-hour care facility.  For more information and definitions of relationships, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Legal Guardian
columns.description: The number of perpetrators (unique count) with a relationship to their victim(s) of legal guardian, which is defined as an adult person who has been given legal custody and guardianship of a minor. For more information and definitions of relationships, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Other Professional
columns.description: The number of perpetrators (unique count) with a relationship to their victim(s) of other professional, which is defined as a person who had contact with the child as part of his or her job, but the profession is not listed as one of the NCANDS codes. For more information and definitions of relationships, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Unmarried Partner of Parent
columns.description: The number of perpetrators (unique count) with a relationship to their victim(s) of unmarried partner of parent, which is defined as someone who has an intimate relationship with the parent and lives in the household with the parent of the maltreated child. For more information and definitions of relationships, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Unknown
columns.description: The number of perpetrators (unique count) with a relationship to their victim(s) of unknown, which is used when the state may collect data on this variable, but the data for this particular report or child were not captured or are missing. For more information and definitions of relationships, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Total Perpetrators
columns.description: The number of perpetrators (unique count) in each state. For more information and definitions of relationships, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Relative
columns.description: The number of perpetrators (unique count) with a relationship to their victim(s) of relative, which is defined as a nonparental family member. For more information and definitions of relationships, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Multiple Relationships
columns.description: The number of perpetrators (unique count) with multiple relationships to their victim(s), which is defined as a perpetrator with two or more relationships to their victims. For more information and definitions of relationships, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Parent
columns.description: The number of perpetrators (unique count) with a relationship to their victim(s) of parent, which is defined as the birth mother or father, adoptive mother or father, or stepmother or stepfather of the child. For more information and definitions of relationships, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

description: The numbers of single perpetrator relationships (unique count) are counted once for each relationship category. Perpetrators with two or more relationships are counted in the multiple relationship category. Numbers are for the most recent federal fiscal year for which data are available. To view more National Child Abuse and Neglect Data System (NCANDS) findings, click link to summary page below: https://healthdata.gov/stories/s/kaeg-w7jc

For more information and definitions of victims, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

name: FY 2024 HHS Contingency Staffing Plan for a Lapse in Appropriation
description: As shown on the summary table, HHS’ contingency plans for agency operations in the absence of appropriations will lead to retaining 51,901 staff and furloughing 35,074 staff as of day two of a funding hiatus. The sum of fatalities (unique count) reported in the Child File and Agency File for federal fiscal year 2019. A child fatality is defined as the death of a child as a result of abuse and neglect, because either an injury resulting from the abuse and neglect was the cause of death, or abuse and neglect were contributing factors to the cause of death. For more information and definitions of Child File, Agency File, child fatalities, child maltreatment, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

name: Maltreatment Types of Victims
provider: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services / ACF

columns.name: Other Only Percent
columns.description: The percentage of victims (unique count) with the “other” maltreatment type (duplicate count). For more information and definitions of maltreatment types, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Unknown Only Percent
columns.description: The percentage of victims (unique count) with the unknown maltreatment type (duplicate count). For the definitions of dispositions, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Multiple Maltreatment Types
columns.description: The percentage of victims (unique count) with the multiple maltreatment type (duplicate count). For the more information and definitions of maltreatment types, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Neglect Only
columns.description: The number of maltreatment types (duplicate count) substantiated as neglect, which is defined as the failure by the caregiver to provide needed, age-appropriate care although financially able to do so or offered financial or other means to do so. For more information and definitions of maltreatment types, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Other Only
columns.description: The number of maltreatment types (duplicate count) substantiated as “other,” which is used when the state coding for this field is not one of the codes in the NCANDS record layout.  For more information and definitions of maltreatment types, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Physical Abuse Only
columns.description: The number of maltreatment types (duplicate count) substantiated as physical abuse, which is defined as physical acts that caused or could have caused physical injury to a child. For more information and definitions of maltreatment types, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Sexual Abuse Only
columns.description: The number of maltreatment types (duplicate count) substantiated as sexual abuse, which is defined as the involvement of the child in sexual activity to provide sexual gratification or financial benefit to the perpetrator, including contacts for sexual purposes, molestation, statutory rape, prostitution, pornography, exposure, incest, or other sexually exploitative activities. For the more information and definitions of maltreatment types, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Sexual Abuse Only Percent
columns.description: The percentage of victims (unique count) with the sexual abuse maltreatment type (duplicate count). For the more information and definitions of maltreatment types, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Sex Trafficking Only Percent
columns.description: The percentage of victims (unique count) with the sexual trafficking maltreatment type (duplicate count). For the more information and definitions of maltreatment types, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Multiple Maltreatment Types Percent
columns.description: The percentage of victims (unique count) with the multiple maltreatment types (duplicate count). For the more information and definitions of maltreatment types, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Sex Trafficking Only
columns.description: The percentage of victims (unique count) with the sex trafficking maltreatment type (duplicate count). For the more information and definitions of maltreatment types, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Unknown Only
columns.description: The number of maltreatment types (duplicate count) that were not known or are missing. For the definitions of dispositions, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Psychological Maltreatment Only Percent
columns.description: The percentage of victims (unique count) with the psychological maltreatment, maltreatment type (duplicate count). For more information and definitions of maltreatment types, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Total Victims
columns.description: The total number of child maltreatment victims (unique count) reported to NCANDS for federal fiscal year 2019. In NCANDS, a victim is defined as a child for whom the state determined at least one maltreatment was substantiated or indicated. This includes a child who died of child abuse and neglect. For more information and definitions of maltreatment types, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Total Victims Percent
columns.description: summed maltreatment types percentages.  For the more information and definitions of maltreatment types, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Medical Neglect Only
columns.description: The number of maltreatment types (duplicate count) substantiated as medical neglect, which is defined as failure of the caregiver to provide for the appropriate health care of the child although financially able to do so, or offered financial or other resources to do so. For more information and definitions of maltreatment types, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Psychological Maltreatment Only
columns.description: The number of maltreatment types (duplicate count) substantiated as psychological maltreatment, which is defined as acts or omissions—other than physical abuse or sexual abuse—that caused or could have caused—conduct, cognitive, affective, or other behavioral or mental disorders. For more information and definitions of maltreatment types, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: State
columns.description: The primary unit from which child maltreatment data are collected. This includes all 50 states, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and the District of Columbia. The state column also includes a national row which displays the total sum, percentage, or rate for only those states included in the analysis.

columns.name: Medical Neglect Only Percent

columns.name: Neglect Only Percent

description: The number of maltreatment types (duplicate count) are counted once for each substantiated maltreatment, but only once per category, by state. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #4a, and reflects occupancy levels for beds reported in #3c. This field is required as of 2/2/2022.

columns.name: total_staffed_pediatric_icu_beds
columns.description: Total number of pediatric ICU beds in the facility that are currently set-up, staffed and are or could be used for a patient within the reporting period. This count includes occupied and unoccupied ICU beds, including any ICU beds that are, or could be, staffed and used for a pediatric patient. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #3c and #5a. Any beds counted in #5c should NOT be counted in #5b. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. Note: All pediatric ICU beds should be considered, regardless of the unit on which the bed is housed. This includes ICU beds located in non-ICU locations, such as mixed acuity units.

description: After May 3, 2024, this dataset and webpage will no longer be updated because hospitals are no longer required to report data on COVID-19 hospital admissions, and hospital capacity and occupancy data, to HHS through CDC’s National Healthcare Safety Network. Data voluntarily reported to NHSN after May 1, 2024, will be available starting May 10, 2024, at COVID Data Tracker Hospitalizations. The following dataset provides state-aggregated data for hospital utilization. These are derived from report

columns.name: staffed_pediatric_icu_bed_occupancy_coverage
columns.description: Total number of set-up and staffed pediatric ICU beds occupied by a patient (coverage). This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is subset of #4c and #6a. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. Note: All occupied pediatric ICU beds should be considered, regardless of the unit on which the bed is housed. This includes ICU beds located in non-ICU locations, such as mixed acuity units.

columns.name: total_staffed_pediatric_icu_beds_coverage
columns.description: Total number of pediatric ICU beds in the facility that are currently set-up, staffed and are or could be used for a patient within the reporting period (coverage). This count includes occupied and unoccupied ICU beds, including any ICU beds that are, or could be, staffed and used for a pediatric patient. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #3c and #5a. Any beds counted in #5c should NOT be counted in #5b. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. Note: All pediatric ICU beds should be considered, regardless of the unit on which the bed is housed. This includes ICU beds located in non-ICU locations, such as mixed acuity units.

columns.name: learning_modality
columns.description: The learning modality of a given school district which includes in-person, remote, or hybrid learning. In-Person: All schools within the district offer face-to-face instruction 5 days per week to all students at all available grade levels. Remote: Schools within the district do not offer face-to-face instruction; all learning is conducted online/remotely to all students at all available grade levels. Hybrid: Schools within the district offer a combination of in-person and remote learning; face-to-face instruction is offered less than 5 days per week, or only to a subset of students.

name: Project Tycho ® Level 1 Data
description: US weekly Nationally Notifiable Disease Surveillance Data from 1888 to 2013. name: Child Fatalities by Submission Type
provider: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services / ACF

columns.name: Child  Fatalities Reported in the Child File
columns.description: The number of child fatalities (unique count) that were reported by each state in its federal fiscal year 2019 Child File data submission. The Child File is an electronic file of child-specific records for each report of alleged child abuse and neglect that received a CPS response. A child fatality is defined as the death of a child as a result of abuse and neglect, because either an injury resulting from the abuse and neglect was the cause of death, or abuse and neglect were contributing factors to the cause of death. For more information and definitions of Child File, child fatalities, child maltreatment, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Total Child Fatalities
columns.description: The sum of fatalities (unique count) reported by each state in its Child File and Agency File for federal fiscal year 2019. A child fatality is defined as the death of a child as a result of abuse and neglect, because either an injury resulting from the abuse and neglect was the cause of death, or abuse and neglect were contributing factors to the cause of death. For more information and definitions of child fatalities, child maltreatment, federal fiscal year and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Child Fatality Rates per 100,000 Children
columns.description: The frequency by each state of child deaths due to maltreatment per 100,000 children in the population younger than 18 years. The state population estimates are from the U.S. Census Bureau. For the definitions of child fatalities and the population estimates used to calculate the rates, see the Glossary and State Characteristics appendixes of Child Maltreatment 2019.

columns.name: State
columns.description: The primary unit from which child maltreatment data are collected. This includes all 50 states, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and the District of Columbia. The state column also includes a national row which displays the total sum, percentage, or rate for only those states included in the analysis.

columns.name: Child Fatalities Reported in the Agency File
columns.description: The number of child fatalities (unique count) that were reported by each state in its federal fiscal year 2019 Agency File data submission. The Agency File includes aggregate data that are often gathered from agencies external to CPS. A child fatality is defined as the death of a child as a result of abuse and neglect, because either an injury resulting from the abuse and neglect was the cause of death, or abuse and neglect were contributing factors to the cause of death. For more information and definitions of Agency File, child fatalities, child maltreatment, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

description: Counts and rates of child fatalities by file submission type for the most recent federal fiscal year for which data are available. To view more National Child Abuse and Neglect Data System (NCANDS) findings, click link to summary page below: https://healthdata.gov/stories/s/kaeg-w7jc

name: Children by Disposition
provider: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services / ACF

columns.name: Closed With No Finding
columns.description: The number of children (duplicate count) who received a disposition that does not conclude with a specific finding because the CPS response could not be completed. For the definitions of dispositions, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Unsubstantiated
columns.description: The number of children (duplicate count) who received an investigation disposition that concludes there was not sufficient evidence under state law to conclude or suspect that the child was maltreated or at-risk of being maltreated. For more information and definitions of dispositions, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Other
columns.description: The number of children (duplicate count) who received a disposition of “other,” which is used by states if none of the other dispositions are applicable. For the definitions of dispositions, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Unknown
columns.description: The number of children (duplicate count) for whom a disposition is unknown or missing. For the definitions of dispositions, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: State
columns.description: The primary unit from which child maltreatment data are collected. This includes all 50 states, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and the District of Columbia. The state column also includes a national row which displays the total sum, percentage, or rate for only those states included in the analysis.

columns.name: Alternative Response
columns.description: The number of children (duplicate count) who received the provision of a response other than an investigation that determines if a child or family needs services. A determination of maltreatment is not made and a perpetrator is not determined. For more information and definitions of dispositions, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Indicated
columns.description: The number of children (duplicate count) who received a disposition that concludes maltreatment could not be substantiated under state law or policy, but there was a reason to suspect that at least one child may have been maltreated or was at-risk of maltreatment. For more information and definitions of dispositions, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Intentionally False
columns.description: The number of children (duplicate count) who received a disposition that concludes the person who made the allegation of maltreatment knew that the allegation was not true. For more information and definitions of dispositions, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Substantiated
columns.description: The number of children (duplicate count) who received an investigation disposition that concludes the allegation of maltreatment or risk of maltreatment was supported or founded by state law or policy. For more information and definitions of dispositions, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: No Alleged Maltreatment
columns.description: The number of children (duplicate count) who received a disposition, but were not the subject of an allegation or any finding of maltreatment. For the definitions of dispositions, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Total Children
columns.description: The summed number of children (duplicate count) counted once for each disposition. For the definitions of dispositions, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

description: The numbers of children (duplicate count) are counted once for each investigation response or alternative response that reached a disposition (finding) for the most recent federal fiscal year for which data are available. *11/29/2021: Added column including year in which data was collected. To view more National Child Abuse and Neglect Data System (NCANDS) findings, click link to summary page below: https://healthdata.gov/stories/s/kaeg-w7jc

name: Children Who Received an Investigation or Alternative Response
provider: U.S. Department of Health & Human Services / ACF

columns.description: The number of children (unique count) who received investigation responses or alternative responses from child protective services agencies and were reported to NCANDS for federal fiscal year 2017. For more information and definitions of investigation responses, alternative responses, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

The number of children (unique count) who received investigation responses or alternative responses from child protective services agencies and were reported to NCANDS for federal fiscal year 2016. For more information and definitions of investigation responses, alternative responses, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

The number of children (unique count) who received investigation responses or alternative responses from child protective services agencies and were reported to NCANDS for federal fiscal year 2015. For more information and definitions of investigation responses, alternative responses, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: State
columns.description: The primary unit from which child maltreatment data are collected. This includes all 50 states, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and the District of Columbia. The state column also includes a national row which displays the total sum, percentage, or rate for only those states included in the analysis.

The number of children (unique count) who received investigation responses or alternative responses from child protective services agencies and were reported to NCANDS for federal fiscal year 2018. For more information and definitions of investigation responses, alternative responses, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: Percent Change from 2015 to 2019
columns.description: The percent change was calculated by subtracting 2015 data from 2019 data, dividing the result by 2015 data, and multiplying by 100. A state must have reported data for both 2015 and 2019 to have a percent change calculated.

The number of children (unique count) who received investigation responses or alternative responses from child protective services agencies and were reported to NCANDS for federal fiscal year 2019. For more information and definitions of investigation responses, alternative responses, federal fiscal year, and unique and duplicate counts, see the Glossary in the Child Maltreatment 2019 report.

columns.name: 2019 Rate per 1,000 Children
columns.description: The frequency for each state of children who received investigation responses or alternative responses per 1,000 children younger than 18 years in the population for 2019. The state population estimates are from the U.S. Census Bureau. For the definitions of investigation responses, alternative responses, and the population estimates used to calculate the rates, see the Glossary and State Characteristics appendixes of Child Maltreatment 2019.

columns.name: 2017 Rate per 1,000 Children
columns.description: The frequency for each state of children who received investigation responses or alternative responses per 1,000 children younger than 18 years in the population for 2017. The state population estimates are from the U.S. Census Bureau. For the definitions of investigation responses, alternative responses, and the population estimates used to calculate the rates, see the Glossary and State Characteristics appendixes of Child Maltreatment 2019.

columns.name: 2018 Rate per 1,000 Children
columns.description: The frequency for each state of children who received investigation responses or alternative responses per 1,000 children younger than 18 years in the population for 2018. The state population estimates are from the U.S. Census Bureau. For the definitions of investigation responses, alternative responses, and the population estimates used to calculate the rates, see the Glossary and State Characteristics appendixes of Child Maltreatment 2019.

columns.name: 2015 Rate per 1,000 Children
columns.description: The frequency for each state of children who received investigation responses or alternative responses per 1,000 children younger than 18 years in the population for 2015. The state population estimates are from the U.S. Census Bureau. For the definitions of investigation responses, alternative responses, and the population estimates used to calculate the rates, see the Glossary and State Characteristics appendixes of Child Maltreatment 2019.

columns.name: 2016 Rate per 1,000 Children
columns.description: The frequency for each state of children who received investigation responses or alternative responses per 1,000 children younger than 18 years in the population for 2016. The state population estimates are from the U.S. Census Bureau. For the definitions of investigation responses, alternative responses, and the population estimates used to calculate the rates, see the Glossary and State Characteristics appendixes of Child Maltreatment 2019.

description: The goal of the Monthly Outcome Survey (MOS) Small Area Estimations (SAE) is to generate estimates of the proportions of adults, by county and month, who were in the population of interest for the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services’ (HHS) We Can Do This COVID-19 Public Education Campaign. These data are designed to be used by practitioners and researchers to understand how county-level COVID-19 updated (bivalent) vaccination hesitancy changed over time in the United States.

description: The Monthly Outcome Survey (MOS) was designed to assess COVID-19 vaccine uptake as well as beliefs, intentions, and behaviors relevant to COVID-19 vaccination at a point in time. The survey fielded on a monthly basis from January 2021 to April 2023. When the MOS first launched, it focused on the primary series of COVID-19 vaccines; in later waves, it was expanded to assess parents’ intentions to get their children vaccinated or boosted and to track booster and updated vaccine uptake and readines

columns.name: staffed_adult_icu_bed_occupancy
columns.description: Total number of staffed adult ICU beds that are occupied. This is a subset of #4b and #6a.

columns.name: previous_day_admission_adult_covid_confirmed_40_49
columns.description: Enter the number of patients by age bracket who were admitted to an adult inpatient bed on the previous calendar day who had laboratory-confirmed COVID19 at the time of admission. This is a subset of #9b. See Appendix D for the definition of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19.

columns.name: on_hand_supply_of_surgical_masks_in_units
columns.description: Optional / Inactive

columns.name: able_to_maintain_ventilator_3day_supplies
columns.description: (Y, N, N/A) Enter Y if your facility is able to maintain at least a 3-day supply of N95 respirators. Enter N if your facility is not able to maintain at least a 3-day supply of N95 respirators. Enter N/A if N95 respirators are not relevant at your facility.

columns.name: total_staffed_pediatric_icu_beds
columns.description: Total number of pediatric ICU beds in the facility that are currently set-up, staffed and are or could be used for a patient within the reporting period. This count includes occupied and unoccupied ICU beds, including any ICU beds that are, or could be, staffed and used for a pediatric patient. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #3c and #5a. Any beds counted in #5c should NOT be counted in #5b. This field is required as of 2/2/2022. Note: All pediatric ICU beds should be considered, regardless of the unit on which the bed is housed. This includes ICU beds located in non-ICU locations, such as mixed acuity units.

columns.name: all_adult_hospital_inpatient_bed_occupied
columns.description: Total number of staffed adult inpatient beds that are occupied. This is a subset of #4a, and reflects occupancy levels for beds reported in #3b.

This includes all overflow, observation, and active surge/expansion beds used for inpatients. This includes ICU beds. Include any surge/hallway/overflow beds that are open for use for a patient, regardless of whether they are occupied or available.

columns.description: Enter Y if you anticipate a critical staffing shortage within a week. Enter N if you do not anticipate a staffing shortage within a week. If you do not report this value, the default is N. If you have a shortage, report Y until the shortage is resolved. Each facility should identify staffing shortages based on their facility needs and internal policies for staffing ratios. The use of temporary staff does not count as a staffing shortage if staffing ratios are met according to the facility’s needs and internal policies for staffing ratios.

columns.description: Total number of set-up and staffed inpatient pediatric beds that are occupied by a patient. Includes both PICU and med-surge beds (beds in which medical or surgical pediatric patients may be routinely placed). Include any occupied surge/hallway/overflow beds that are open for use. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #4a, and reflects occupancy levels for beds reported in #3c. This field is required as of 2/2/2022.

columns.name: all_pediatric_inpatient_beds
columns.description: Total number of pediatric beds in the facility that are currently set-up, staffed and able to be used for a patient within the reporting period. This count includes occupied and unoccupied inpatient pediatric beds including both PICU and med-surge beds (beds in which medical or surgical pediatric patients may be routinely placed). Include any surge/hallway/overflow beds that are open for use for a patient, regardless of whether they are occupied or available. This count excludes NICU, newborn nursery beds, and outpatient surgery beds. This is a subset of #3a. This field is required as of 2/2/2022.

columns.name: previous_day_covid_ED_visits
columns.description: Enter the total number of ED visits who were seen on the previous calendar day who had a visit related to suspected or laboratory-confirmed COVID-19. Do not count patients who receive a COVID-19 test solely for screening purposes in the absence of COVID-19 symptoms. “Suspected” is defined as a person who is being managed as though he/she has COVID-19 because of signs and symptoms suggestive of COVID-19 but does not have a laboratory-positive COVID-19 test result. See Appendix D for the definition of laboratory-confirmed COVID-19.
